summary.en: A bohemian, beach-side neighborhood known for its nightlife and vibrant cultural atmosphere, perfect for walking.
A fascinating, occasionally chaotic mix of modern mega-city elements intertwined with stunning colonial architecture.
A gritty, chaotic, and major transit hub that offers an undeniably authentic slice of everyday urban life.
An upscale, bustling neighborhood with massive green spaces along the coast, upscale nightlife, and deep historical roots.
A deeply historical and slightly quieter district holding significant archaeological and colonial artifacts.
A densely packed, authentic neighborhood that has become a major hub for incredible, unpretentious local street food.

description.en: An old-school cevichería and huarique that has stood in Barranco for over 35 years, making it a beloved local institution.
An internationally recognized Peruvian dining spot to experience high-end Peruvian gastronomy.
A bright, all-day bakery and café that perfectly fits the growing coffee and pastry scene in Lima.
A classic taberna run by Ricardo Martins where it feels like 'time stands still,' offering a truly authentic local vibe.
A cultural landmark in the bohemian district showcasing a notable collection of art.
A bustling area filled with street vendors and heavy pedestrian traffic, offering a vibrant cultural experience.
A century-old, cash-only Italian-Peruvian café and bar that is a true cult hit among locals and an authentic time capsule.
A stunning example of colonial religious architecture in the historic heart of Lima.
A highly specific regional restaurant that offers a true 'taste of the Andes' right in the middle of the coastal capital.
A massive architectural landmark located directly on the busy Paseo de la Republica.
A unique shopping street where you can hunt for original paintings and antiques.
A massive pre-Inca temple of the Lima culture dating back to 500 AD, located right in the middle of the modern city.
A casual space serving fantastic seafood that evokes the atmosphere of a classic beachside cevichería.
A renowned Nikkei restaurant known for the best integration of Peruvian ingredients with Japanese culinary techniques.
A scenic park located on the cliffs of Miraflores, offering beautiful coastal views.
An excellent local café in Parque Kennedy serving reasonably priced Middle Eastern foods like falafel and shawarma.
A street specifically noted for its unique shopping, where you can find local Peruvian handicrafts.
A monumental Christian cross dating back to the era of the Spanish Conquistadores, offering a piece of 'secret history'.
A major museum housed in the district, perfect for exploring Peru's deep pre-Hispanic history.
A modern huarique featuring only a 12-seat counter to experience ceviche prepared the 'proper Limeño way' right in front of you.
A lunch-only spot where the 'aromas and flavors run deep,' offering an intensely local daytime dining experience.
The only Venezuelan-Peruvian fine dining restaurant in town, offering an incredible and unique culinary experience.
A popular Barranco bar that has shaped a dining and drinking experience built on local spirits and seasonal ingredients.
A unique urban cafe housed inside a beautifully restored antique rail car.
Tucked behind the Institute of Neurological Science, this museum holds the largest display of human brains in Latin America.
A classic and beloved sanguchería specializing in the iconic pan con chicharrón (pork sandwich).
A 'witches market' hidden near a train station, offering a fascinating glimpse into local shamanistic traditions and remedies.
A restaurant with a strong Peruvian identity, offering local meats and ingredients approached with a contemporary twist.
A picantería that is always packed, serving vibrant and authentic dishes from the northern Piurana region.
A fine dining spot that artfully weaves Mediterranean, Asian, and Peruvian influences into its seasonal menu.
This restaurant offers a refined and modern take on Amazonian cuisine, bringing the unique flavors of the jungle to the city.
Located within the National Museum of Archaeology, this room is lined with thousands of elongated human craniums from ancient cultures.
A low-key and unpretentious seafood spot with delicious, daily changing dishes shaped by the seasons and the day's catch.

prompt.en: Capture the essence of this 35-year-old cevichería. Focus on what makes it look like a beloved neighborhood spot.
Take a mouth-watering close-up shot of your plate of ceviche at this old-school huarique.
Photograph the artistic plating of a dish at Central, showcasing its high-end culinary approach.
Capture an image that shows how this internationally recognized restaurant fits into the bohemian district of Barranco.
Capture a photo that highlights the bright, modern interior of Demo, contrasting with its delicious pastries.
Find a vantage point that captures the contrast between Demo's bright, modern style and the historic tabernas of Barranco.
Take a picture that conveys the authentic, time-capsule feeling of this classic local taberna.
Photograph a detail inside La Perlita that you think best represents its authentic, local vibe.
Photograph a piece of art inside the museum that you feel best represents the cultural spirit of Barranco.
Take a photo of the museum's exterior, highlighting its architecture as a cultural landmark.
Capture the energy of the heavy pedestrian traffic and street vendors in Barrio Chino.
Take a picture of the iconic red archway at the entrance to Chinatown.
Take a photo that captures the 'time capsule' feel of this century-old, cash-only Italian-Peruvian café.
Photograph a detail inside Bodega Carbone that makes it feel like a cult hit among locals.
Photograph the stunning colonial facade of the San Francisco Church and Monastery.
Find and capture a specific architectural detail that showcases the building's colonial religious design.
Photograph a dish that looks like it could be a true 'taste of the Andes' in the middle of the capital.
Capture the exterior or interior of Huancahuasi in a way that highlights its specific, regional character.
Take a wide shot of the massive National Stadium, showing its scale against the busy Paseo de la Republica.
Focus on an interesting architectural feature of the stadium's design.
Find and photograph an interesting antique or original painting in one of the shops on this unique street.
Capture the general atmosphere of Av. La Paz, showing the storefronts and unique character of the street.
Capture the juxtaposition of the ancient adobe pyramid of Huaca Pucllana against the backdrop of upscale, modern Miraflores buildings.
Get a close-up shot of the adobe bricks of the pre-Inca temple, focusing on the texture and age.
Take a photo that captures the casual, classic beachside cevichería atmosphere of La Mar.
Focus on a dish of fantastic seafood, making it the star of your photo.
Photograph a dish at Maido that best shows the integration of Peruvian ingredients with Japanese techniques.
Capture the ambiance of this renowned Nikkei restaurant.
Take a scenic photo from the park that captures the view of the coast from the cliffs.
Photograph the famous 'El Beso' (The Kiss) statue at the center of the park.
Get a shot of your falafel or shawarma sandwich with the bustling scenery of Parque Kennedy in the background.
Capture the fast-paced, casual vibe of this excellent local café in the middle of the park.
Find a local Peruvian handicraft for sale on Av. La Marina and take a detailed photo of it.
Take a picture that captures the atmosphere of shopping for unique, local goods on this street.
Go on a scavenger hunt to locate the exact site of the Cruz del Viajero and photograph its monumental scale.
Take a photo that emphasizes the age and historical significance of this cross from the era of the Spanish Conquistadores.
Photograph an artifact in the museum that you find most fascinating from Peru's deep pre-Hispanic history.
Capture the exterior of the museum, showing how this major cultural institution is housed within the district.
Secure one of the 12 seats and capture a close-up, action-shot of the chef preparing your ceviche at the counter.
Take a photo of your finished dish, trying to make it look as fresh and authentic as possible.
Capture an image that conveys the 'aromas and flavors run deep' description of this lunch-only spot.
Photograph the bustling, intensely local daytime dining experience inside La Picantería.
Find the most vibrant piece of street art in Barranco and take a creative photo of it.
Take a selfie in front of a colorful colonial building that expresses Barranco's artistic vibe.
Capture a photo of the Pacific Ocean from one of Barranco's cliffside viewpoints (malecón).
Discover and photograph a quiet, hidden courtyard or alleyway off the main streets.
Find a local artisan shop and take a picture of a unique handmade item.
Take an artistic, top-down photo of your coffee or drink at a local cafe.
Take a photo of the famous Puente de los Suspiros. Try to capture it from a unique angle.
Take a selfie where you mimic the pose of a character in a nearby mural or statue.
Find an interesting architectural detail on a historic casona (mansion) and get a close-up shot.
Capture a photo of a musician or artist performing on the street.
Photograph the facade or entrance of one of Barranco's many art galleries.
Find the most beautiful flower or plant in a local park or garden and take a picture.
Take a candid photo that captures the relaxed, bohemian atmosphere of the neighborhood.
Photograph a delicious-looking dessert or pastry from a local bakery.
Find and photograph a historical plaque or sign that tells a story about the area.
Take a photo of one of the ornate, historic wooden balconies that are famous in Centro Histórico.
Take a selfie with the grand cathedral or the Government Palace in the background at the main square.
Capture the iconic yellow color of the historic buildings that dominate the city center.
Find a street vendor and take a delicious-looking photo of a local snack like picarones or anticuchos.
Photograph the intricate architectural details of one of the many historic church facades.
Go on a hunt for the oldest, most interesting-looking door you can find and snap a picture.
Visit a historic bar and take a photo of a freshly made Pisco Sour.
Capture the energy of the bustling Jirón de la Unión pedestrian street.
Find a statue of a historical figure and take a selfie mimicking their pose.
Step inside a colonial building and photograph its quiet, beautiful interior courtyard.
Take a photo of the Government Palace, trying to catch the formal Changing of the Guard ceremony if you can.
Get a low-angle shot of one of the main plazas, capturing the flocks of pigeons and the grand architecture.
Find the entrance to Chinatown (Barrio Chino) and photograph the distinctive archway or a nearby restaurant sign.
Photograph the historic Casa de la Literatura Peruana (House of Peruvian Literature).
Capture a photo that shows the contrast between a historic colonial building and modern city life (e.g., cars, people on phones).
Capture the chaotic, vibrant energy of the Gamarra textile market in a single photo.
Find a stall with colorful fabrics and take a selfie with your favorite pattern.
Photograph a 'menu del día' sign outside a local restaurant, showcasing an authentic lunch special.
Take a candid photo of vendors and shoppers interacting in the marketplace.
Find a street food cart and take an action shot of the chef at work.
Photograph a line of mannequins outside a clothing store, capturing the local fashion.
Take a picture of a hearty, traditional Peruvian meal from a local eatery.
Take a photo of the exterior of the National Stadium, home of Peruvian football.
Capture the constant motion of buses, taxis, and mototaxis that characterizes the neighborhood.
Look for an unexpected piece of street art or a mural on a side street and photograph it.
Get a close-up shot of the patterns and textures of a stack of textiles for sale.
Find a quiet residential street and capture a slice of everyday local life.
With permission, take a friendly selfie with a local shopkeeper or vendor.
Photograph an interesting or vintage-looking storefront sign.
Near the Mercado de Brujas, find and photograph an object or stall that looks particularly mysterious.
Capture a photo of the paragliders soaring above the Costa Verde cliffs.
Find one of the famous cats of Parque Kennedy and take its portrait.
Take a selfie with the iconic 'El Beso' statue in the Parque del Amor.
Order some ceviche and take a mouth-watering, artistic photo before you dig in.
Photograph the Huaca Pucllana pyramid with the modern buildings of Miraflores in the background.
Head to the beach and capture a photo of a surfer riding a wave.
Take a photo of the sunset over the Pacific Ocean from the malecón (boardwalk).
Find a piece of public art or a sculpture in a park and take a creative selfie with it.
Discover and photograph one of the hidden staircases that lead from the cliffs down to the coast.
Find a trendy cafe and capture its stylish interior or a beautifully presented coffee.
Take a photo that showcases the beautiful, well-maintained gardens and parks of the district.
Find a shop selling high-quality alpaca goods and take a photo of the softest-looking sweater.
Photograph a modern high-rise building with interesting architecture.
Capture the view from the Larcomar shopping center, built into the side of the cliff.
In or around Parque Kennedy, find an artist selling their paintings and photograph their work.
Take a grand photo of the exterior of the Museo Nacional de Arqueología, Antropología e Historia del Perú.
Step into a classic 'taberna antigua' and take a selfie in its historic, atmospheric interior.
Capture a photo of the historic Plaza Bolívar, including the monument to the liberators.
Find a beautifully preserved colonial-era house and photograph its unique architecture.
Take a picture of a quiet, tree-lined residential street that shows the tranquil side of the neighborhood.
Find a street or monument named after a Peruvian hero and take a photo of it.
Photograph a classic criollo dish being served at a traditional local restaurant.
Find a grand, colonial-style doorway and take a selfie in front of it.
Photograph a classic neighborhood bodega (corner store).
Get a close-up shot of the intricate woodwork on a traditional colonial balcony.
Capture the beautiful, bougainvillea-covered entrance to the Museo Larco.
Order a Chilcano, a classic pisco cocktail, and take a refreshing-looking photo of it.
Find a tranquil scene in a local park or plaza and capture the peaceful atmosphere.
Find and photograph a historical plaque that explains the significance of a local building or site.
Find the famous, ancient Ombú tree in the district and take a photo showcasing its massive size.
Capture a wide shot of the interior of the Mercado de Surquillo, showing the vibrant chaos and variety of products.
Find the most unusual-looking fruit at a market stall and take a selfie with it.
Find a ceviche stall or restaurant and take a photo of the chef preparing the dish.
Photograph a stall's impressive display of the many different varieties of Peruvian potatoes.
Discover a stall specializing in ingredients from the Amazon jungle and photograph its unique offerings.
Find a small, unassuming eatery (a 'huarique') and photograph its exterior.
Take a picture of a handwritten 'menu del día' board advertising the day's lunch special.
Capture a photo of the fresh catch of the day at the seafood section of the market.
Take an action shot of fresh juice being made at a market stall.
Take a candid photo that captures the essence of locals doing their daily grocery shopping.
Photograph a butcher at work or their display of meats.
Find a stall selling medicinal herbs and plants and photograph the interesting display.
Buy a bag of canchita (toasted corn) and take a selfie while enjoying the snack.
Capture a photo showing the contrast between the traditional market and the modern high-rises of neighboring Miraflores.
Find a stall with a vibrant display of different types of Peruvian ají (chiles) and take a colorful close-up.
